message: Planned speech acts for 10 chapters (18 scenes) across 4 episodes

speech_acts.speaker: Archivist
speech_acts.subtext: Frames the crystal fragments as a cosmic duty — but what the archivist actually wants from Elara remains opaque
speech_acts.expected_effect: Elara learns of the quest and the cabal's plan; a faint hope stirs beneath her paralysis

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: Claims powerlessness and indifference as a shield — she is terrified of failing again and losing the last shard of her identity
speech_acts.expected_effect: The archivist must find a stronger lever; Elara's deflection signals she is not yet closed to persuasion

speech_acts.speaker: Archivist
speech_acts.subtext: Invokes atonement deliberately — the archivist knows guilt is Elara's only remaining fuel and is willing to use it
speech_acts.expected_effect: Elara's dormant hope and guilt conflict; she does not answer aloud but her scar pulses

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: She asks whether she can trust the whispers, but what she really asks is whether she is worth saving — a test of the archivist's intent
speech_acts.expected_effect: The archivist must reframe her self-doubt as the very reason she must act

speech_acts.speaker: Archivist
speech_acts.subtext: Orders her to rise and seek the map, testing whether any fight remains beneath the guilt
speech_acts.expected_effect: Elara bristles at being commanded, revealing her autonomy need is intact beneath the despair

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: Invokes the three dead acolytes as a reason she cannot act — using guilt as a wall against the risk of hoping again
speech_acts.expected_effect: Archivist presses harder, knowing the guilt-wall is also a door

speech_acts.speaker: Archivist
speech_acts.subtext: Drops that her experiment was sabotaged, offering righteous anger as a cleaner motivator than guilt
speech_acts.expected_effect: Elara's paralysis fractures into shock and the first flicker of fury

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: Asks for proof the map is not a trap, but what she is really bargaining for is certainty — which neither can provide
speech_acts.expected_effect: Archivist deflects; Elara's trust remains conditional, her vigilance sharpens

speech_acts.speaker: Archivist
speech_acts.subtext: Responds that trust is earned through action, not given in advance — may be withholding, may simply not know
speech_acts.expected_effect: Elara is frustrated but continues; the archivist's credibility is neither confirmed nor refuted

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: Mutters that she must be mad to follow a dead voice — testing her own sanity aloud
speech_acts.expected_effect: Articulating doubt externalizes it; she feels slightly more in control for having named it

speech_acts.speaker: Cabal Hunter
speech_acts.subtext: Orders surrender with the casual confidence of someone who expects a broken mage to comply — underestimating her
speech_acts.expected_effect: Elara's pride is stung; she realizes they see her as the woman she used to be, not who she is becoming

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: Taunts them for sending a squad after a 'broken mage' — bravado masking terror, testing their competence
speech_acts.expected_effect: Hunter is angered into rashness; underestimation becomes overreach

speech_acts.speaker: Cabal Hunter
speech_acts.subtext: Promises a fate worse than death, revealing they have orders to make an example of her
speech_acts.expected_effect: Elara's survival instinct overrides pride; she shifts from confrontation to escape

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: Throws debris and flees, shouting a taunt over her shoulder — flight framed as dismissiveness
speech_acts.expected_effect: Hunter pursues in fury, abandoning tactical discipline for revenge

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: Accuses the archivist of leading her into a trap as the map fails to match the terrain — what she really fears is that she was never meant to succeed
speech_acts.expected_effect: Archivist must defend its credibility or let the accusation stand

speech_acts.speaker: Archivist
speech_acts.subtext: Admits the map was stolen, not created, and may have been tampered with — distances itself from responsibility without confirming or denying
speech_acts.expected_effect: Elara's paranoia spikes; she can no longer trust the map, only herself

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: Whispers that she should have stayed in exile — self-preservation screaming at her to flee — but keeps walking
speech_acts.expected_effect: Action contradicts words; she proves to herself that she is committed even when terrified

speech_acts.speaker: Cabal Ambusher
speech_acts.subtext: Reveals the map was a trap all along, aiming to break her spirit before the blades finish the work
speech_acts.expected_effect: Elara's worst fear is confirmed — but instead of freezing, desperation sharpens her instincts

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: Claims she has survived worse — a lie feeding her own courage as much as a taunt to the enemy
speech_acts.expected_effect: Ambusher scoffs and presses the attack, but Elara's defiance unsettles them

speech_acts.speaker: Cabal Ambusher
speech_acts.subtext: Calls her a fool for trusting a dead traitor's map — inadvertently reveals the archivist was no ally of the cabal either
speech_acts.expected_effect: Elara files away the implication mid-combat: the archivist and cabal are enemies, not co-conspirators

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: She reads the records aloud to confirm what she is seeing — the words are not for the archivist but for herself, to make the truth real
speech_acts.expected_effect: The guilt she has carried for years is replaced by shock; she needs the archivist to confirm she is not misreading

speech_acts.speaker: Archivist
speech_acts.subtext: Confirms she was sabotaged — and that this is why the archivist chose her: she is the only one with motive and moral claim to stop them
speech_acts.expected_effect: Elara's guilt transmutes to righteous fury; her quest recontextualized from atonement to justice

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: Demands everything the archivist has withheld — she is done being a pawn, even to a dead ally
speech_acts.expected_effect: Archivist concedes it does not know everything; the power dynamic shifts — Elara is no longer a supplicant

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: Vows aloud that she will stop them — not for revenge but for justice. She is trying on the identity of righteous avenger to see if it fits
speech_acts.expected_effect: It fits. She names her new purpose and the name solidifies her resolve

speech_acts.speaker: Cabal Leader
speech_acts.subtext: Welcomes the 'prodigal failure' back, couching a genuine offer of alliance in cruelty — the leader respects Elara's power and wants it, but cannot offer respect openly
speech_acts.expected_effect: Elara must resist the seduction of belonging; the leader's mockery reveals their value system

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: States plainly that she knows the truth — they destroyed her to control the crystal. She wants the leader to acknowledge the betrayal, to validate her pain
speech_acts.expected_effect: Leader is unmoved; the confession is weaponized against her — 'then you know you owe us nothing and can still choose us'

speech_acts.speaker: Cabal Leader
speech_acts.subtext: Argues the crystal can restore her power, that she can have everything back — fundamentally misunderstands that Elara's desire is atonement, not power
speech_acts.expected_effect: Elara realizes the leader's moral language is untranslatable to hers; the offer confirms they have nothing for her

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: Warns she will shatter the crystal before she lets them use it — a commitment spoken aloud that terrifies even her
speech_acts.expected_effect: Leader is genuinely shocked; this outcome was not in their calculations

speech_acts.speaker: Elara Vex
speech_acts.subtext: Chooses to shatter the crystal in silence. The act itself is the speech act — words would diminish it
speech_acts.expected_effect: The blight disperses; Elara loses her remaining power but gains moral clarity and redemption
